title: Where can i find Shinji Ikusei Keikaku walkthrough
display_title: Where can i find Shinji Ikusei Keikaku walkthrough
wikitext:
[]: Where can i find Shinji Ikusei Keikaku walkthrough?             Quote Post by Elhan » Tue Mar 27, 2007 5:49 am please i can't find them on gamefaq.com and i'll be very appreciate if someone who master this game wrote the Walkthrough Please.... TvT (i just want to get married with Rei) TvT

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Tue Mar 27, 2007 1:50 pm Once upon a time, I got sick of being completely reliant on other people to know what any of that mysterioso Japanese writing said. So I learned some. And what empowerment! Seriously, if you care enough about playing these games, you can take the time to learn the two syllaberies, at least.

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Tue Mar 27, 2007 2:16 pm Agreed. Even if you know just hirgana and katakana (like me), it's something/helpful enough. Espeically for those games where the characters speak their lines as well as the dialouge being written.

[]:             Quote Post by Fearthebait » Tue Mar 27, 2007 11:44 pm just wondering, what do you think is the most effective way to begin learning the language? even if its just the basics.

[]:             Quote Post by slothen » Tue Mar 27, 2007 11:47 pm take a class. lol

[]:             Quote Post by BrikHaus » Tue Mar 27, 2007 11:50 pm Fearthebait wrote: just wondering, what do you think is the most effective way to begin learning the language? even if its just the basics.

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Wed Mar 28, 2007 12:30 am Fearthebait wrote: just wondering, what do you think is the most effective way to begin learning the language? even if its just the basics.

[]:             Quote Post by BrikHaus » Wed Mar 28, 2007 8:40 am Reichu wrote: I started learning the syllaberies in the most inefficient way possible, so I really can't say.

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Wed Mar 28, 2007 8:45 am BrikHaus81 wrote: What is the most inefficient way to learn them? Just curious if my method of learning them was just as bad

[]:             Quote Post by BrikHaus » Wed Mar 28, 2007 8:50 am Reichu wrote: BrikHaus81 wrote: What is the most inefficient way to learn them? Just curious if my method of learning them was just as bad

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Wed Mar 28, 2007 10:03 am BrikHaus81 wrote: You definitely win for the most bizarre method of learning them, that's for sure.

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Thu Mar 29, 2007 2:25 pm Speaking if it's basic things and writing in the Kana I can do, just not those "accursed Kanji-things", which I will pratice for "evil purposes"* during Spring Break. Though I only took two quarters of it as well as a conversaional class to make sure I would't forget things, at least I know something. *If all goes well they'll be a new topic post from me in some...section of EMF. Dunno where it would fit yet. ^^;

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Thu Mar 29, 2007 7:01 pm Sailor Star Dust wrote: *If all goes well they'll be a new topic post from me in some...section of EMF. Dunno where it would fit yet. ^^;

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Fri Mar 30, 2007 12:10 pm The only thing I can contribute to the actual topic is somebody (The Bastard King??) claimed their planning on translating the Ikari Shinji Raising Project game. If you search on places like these, you might find something in terms of walkthroughs: http://www.gamefaqs.com/search/index.html?game=evangelion&platform=0 Reichu wrote: among other things. "Thanks for nothing, Professor Paint!"
